headline: Google DeepMind argues LLMs cannot achieve consciousness
body: Google DeepMind published a paper arguing that large language models will never develop consciousness, settling a technical debate about AI cognition. For healthcare AI, this distinction matters: it clarifies that clinical decision support systems operate as sophisticated pattern-matching tools, not thinking entities capable of independent judgment or accountability. This reinforces the regulatory and liability framework healthcare organizations need when deploying AI in workflows like diagnostic assistance or EHR documentation. Healthcare vendors and health systems should use this clarity to focus implementations on augmentation and safety monitoring rather than treating AI as autonomous clinical decision-makers.
